items.title: US and Iran Reach Preliminary Ceasefire Agreement, Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
items.content: The US and Iran reached a preliminary peace agreement on June 15, brokered by Pakistan, to end prolonged conflict. The deal includes an immediate ceasefire, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and lifting of the US naval blockade on Iranian ports. Formal signing expected June 19 in Switzerland. Full normalization of shipping may take weeks due to naval mines. Oil prices expected to ease significantly.

items.title: S&P 500 and Dow Jones Hit Record Highs on AI Rally and Ceasefire Optimism
items.content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at a record high. S&P 500 trading near all-time highs after nine consecutive weekly gains, up 7.7% YTD. Nasdaq Composite up 14.81% YTD as of June 15. AI capital spending and the US-Iran ceasefire deal are the primary catalysts driving the rally.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Falls 1.4% as Investors Take Profits Ahead of China Data
items.content: The Hang Seng Index closed at 24,493.95 on June 16, down 1.4%. The decline followed a two-day rally as investors took profits ahead of key Chinese economic data. Weakness in finance and technology stocks contributed to the pressure. HSI is down 4.45% over the past month.

items.title: US April PCE Inflation Moderates, Core PCE Rises 0.24% Below Forecast
items.content: April PCE Price Index rose 0.40% MoM (below 0.53% forecast), 3.8% YoY (below 3.9% forecast). Core PCE rose 0.24% MoM (below 0.30% forecast), 3.3% YoY (in line). Energy prices remained the primary driver. The moderation supports expectations for the Fed to hold rates steady at the June meeting.

items.title: May CPI Rises 0.5% MoM, 4.2% YoY; Core CPI +0.2%
items.content: The Consumer Price Index for May 2026 rose 0.5% on a seasonally adjusted basis, and 4.2% over the last 12 months. Energy prices drove the increase, with gasoline rising 7.0%. Core CPI (ex food & energy) rose 0.2% MoM. Inflation remains well above the Fed's 2% target.

items.title: May Nonfarm Payrolls +172,000; Unemployment Steady at 4.3%
items.content: US economy added 172,000 jobs in May. Unemployment rate held at 4.3%. Job gains in leisure & hospitality, local government, and healthcare. Employment in financial activities declined. Labor market remains resilient.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 FY27 Beats Estimates on Turnaround, Revenue Up 1.9%
items.content: Best Buy reported Q1 FY27 EPS of $1.28, beating $1.23 consensus. Revenue $8.94B (+1.9% YoY) beat estimates. Comparable sales turned positive at +2%. Full-year FY27 guidance reiterated at $6.30-$6.60 EPS. Consumer electronics demand stabilizing.

items.title: Snowflake Q1 FY27 Massive Beat, Product Revenue Guidance Raised to $5.84B
items.content: Snowflake reported Q1 EPS of $0.39, crushing estimates of $0.14 (+178.57%). Revenue $1.39B beat $1.32B consensus. Raised FY2027 product revenue guidance to $5.84B, projecting 31% YoY growth. AI-driven data cloud demand surging.

items.title: Dollar Tree Q3 Beats, Raises Full-Year Outlook Amid Consumer Resilience
items.content: Dollar Tree reported adjusted EPS of $1.21, beating $1.08 consensus by 11.8%. Revenue $4.75B slightly above estimates. Raised FY2026 adjusted EPS outlook to $5.60-$5.80. Consumer demand at discount retailers remains strong.

items.title: China Retail Sales Fall 0.6% YoY in May, First Contraction Since 2022
items.content: China's retail sales fell 0.6% year-on-year in May 2026, marking the first contraction since December 2022. Industrial production grew 4.5% YoY, beating forecasts. Fixed-asset investment declined 4.1% in first five months. Property sector continues to struggle with widespread price declines.

items.title: China Semiconductor Stocks Rally as IC Exports Surge 83.7% YoY
items.content: China's integrated circuit exports surged 83.7% YoY in first four months of 2026. A-share semiconductor companies reported 124.83% YoY net profit growth in Q1 2026. AI demand driving the semiconductor market to projected $1.511 trillion globally in 2026. CXMT and YMTC gaining ground.

items.title: FOMC Begins Two-Day Meeting; New Chair Kevin Warsh's First Decision Expected as Hold
items.content: The FOMC started its June 16-17 meeting, the first under new Chair Kevin Warsh. Expected to hold rates steady. Markets watching for language shift in the statement, especially whether the previous bias towards rate cuts is removed. With CPI at 4.2% and core PCE at 3.3%, the Fed faces a tough inflation outlook.
